verses.latin_vulgate: In diebus illis ægrotavit Ezechias usque ad mortem : et venit ad eum Isaias filius Amos propheta, dixitque ei : Hæc dicit Dominus Deus : Præcipe domui tuæ : morieris enim tu, et non vives.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בַּיָּמִ֣ים הָהֵ֔ם חָלָ֥ה חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ לָמ֑וּת וַיָּבֹ֣א אֵ֠לָ֠יו יְשַׁעְיָ֨הוּ בֶן־אָמ֜וֹץ הַנָּבִ֗יא וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֵלָ֜יו כֹּֽה־אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ צַ֣ו לְבֵיתֶ֔ךָ כִּ֛י מֵ֥ת אַתָּ֖ה וְלֹ֥א תִֽחְיֶֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: In those days Ezechias was sick unto death: and Isaias, the son of Amos, the prophet, came and said to him: Thus saith the Lord God: Give charge concerning thy house, for thou shalt die, and not llve.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin sairasti Hiskia kuolemallansa, ja propheta Jesaia Amotsin poika tuli hänen tykönsä ja sanoi hänelle: näin sanoo Herra: toimita talos, sillä sinun pitää nyt kuoleman ja ei elämän.
verses.king_james: In those days was Hezekiah sick unto death. And the prophet Isaiah the son of Amoz came to him, and said unto him, Thus saith the Lord, Set thine house in order; for thou shalt die, and not live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui convertit faciem suam ad parietem, et oravit Dominum, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיַּסֵּ֥ב אֶת־פָּנָ֖יו אֶל־הַקִּ֑יר וַיִּ֨תְפַּלֵּ֔ל אֶל־יְהֹוָ֖ה לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he turned his face to the wall, and prayed to the Lord, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta hän käänsi kasvonsa seinän puoleen, rukoili Herraa ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: Then he turned his face to the wall, and prayed unto the Lord, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Obsecro, Domine : memento, quæso, quomodo ambulaverim coram te in veritate, et in corde perfecto, et quod placitum est coram te fecerim. Flevit itaque Ezechias fletu magno.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אָנָּ֣ה יְהֹוָ֗ה זְכׇר־נָ֞א אֵ֣ת אֲשֶׁ֧ר הִתְהַלַּ֣כְתִּי לְפָנֶ֗יךָ בֶּאֱמֶת֙ וּבְלֵבָ֣ב שָׁלֵ֔ם וְהַטּ֥וֹב בְּעֵינֶ֖יךָ עָשִׂ֑יתִי וַיֵּ֥בְךְּ חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ בְּכִ֥י גָדֽוֹל׃ {ס}
verses.douay_rheims: I beseech thee, O Lord, remember how I have walked before thee in truth, and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is pleasing before thee. And Ezechias wept with much weeping.
verses.finnish_biblia: Oi, Herra! muista että minä olen uskollisesti ja vaalla sydämellä vaeltanut sinun edessäs ja tehnyt, mikä sinulle otollinen ollut on! Ja Hiskia itki hartaasti.
verses.king_james: I beseech thee, O Lord, remember now how I have walked before thee in truth and with a perfect heart, and have done that which is good in thy sight. And Hezekiah wept sore.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et antequam egrederetur Isaias mediam partem atrii, factus est sermo Domini ad eum, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיְהִ֣י יְשַֽׁעְיָ֔הוּ לֹ֣א יָצָ֔א (העיר) [חָצֵ֖ר] הַתִּיכֹנָ֑ה וּ֨דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֔ה הָיָ֥ה אֵלָ֖יו לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.douay_rheims: And before Isaias was gone out of the middle of the court, the word of the Lord came to him, saying:
verses.finnish_biblia: Mutta kuin Jesaia ei ollut vielä puoli kaupunkiin käynyt ulos, tuli Herran sana hänen tykönsä ja sanoi:
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, afore Isaiah was gone out into the middle court, that the word of the Lord came to him, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Revertere, et dic Ezechiæ duci populi mei : Hæc dicit Dominus Deus David patris tui : Audivi orationem tuam, et vidi lacrimas tuas, et ecce sanavi te : die tertio ascendes templum Domini.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שׁ֣וּב וְאָמַרְתָּ֞ אֶל־חִזְקִיָּ֣הוּ נְגִיד־עַמִּ֗י כֹּֽה־אָמַ֤ר יְהֹוָה֙ אֱלֹהֵי֙ דָּוִ֣ד אָבִ֔יךָ שָׁמַ֙עְתִּי֙ אֶת־תְּפִלָּתֶ֔ךָ רָאִ֖יתִי אֶת־דִּמְעָתֶ֑ךָ הִנְנִי֙ רֹ֣פֶא לָ֔ךְ בַּיּוֹם֙ הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֔י תַּעֲלֶ֖ה בֵּ֥ית יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Go back, and tell Ezechias, the captain of my people: Thus saith the Lord, the God of David, thy father: I have heard thy prayer, and I have seen thy tears: and behold I have healed thee: on the third day thou shalt go up to the temple of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Palaja ja sano Hiskialle, kansani päämiehelle: näin sanoo Herra sinun isäs Davidin Jumala: minä olen kuullut sinun rukoukses ja nähnyt sinun kyyneles: katso, minä parannan sinun: kolmantena päivänä pitää sinun menemän ylös Herran huoneesen.
verses.king_james: Turn again, and tell Hezekiah the captain of my people, Thus saith the Lord, the God of David thy father, I have heard thy prayer, I have seen thy tears: behold, I will heal thee: on the third day thou shalt go up unto the house of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et addam diebus tuis quindecim annos : sed et de manu regis Assyriorum liberabo te, et civitatem hanc : et protegam urbem istam propter me, et propter David servum meum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֹסַפְתִּ֣י עַל־יָמֶ֗יךָ חֲמֵ֤שׁ עֶשְׂרֵה֙ שָׁנָ֔ה וּמִכַּ֤ף מֶֽלֶךְ־אַשּׁוּר֙ אַצִּ֣ילְךָ֔ וְאֵ֖ת הָעִ֣יר הַזֹּ֑את וְגַנּוֹתִי֙ עַל־הָעִ֣יר הַזֹּ֔את לְמַֽעֲנִ֔י וּלְמַ֖עַן דָּוִ֥ד עַבְדִּֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And I will add to thy days fifteen years: and I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of the Assyrians, and I will protect this city for my own sake, and for David, my servant's sake.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja minä lisään sinun ikääs viisitoistakymmentä ajastaikaa, ja vapahdan sinun ja tämän kaupungin Assyrian kuninkaan kädestä, ja varjelen tämän kaupungin minun tähteni ja minun palveliani Davidin tähden.
verses.king_james: And I will add unto thy days fifteen years; and I will deliver thee and this city out of the hand of the king of Assyria; and I will defend this city for mine own sake, and for my servant David’s sake.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixitque Isaias : Afferte massam ficorum. Quam cum attulissent, et posuissent super ulcus ejus, curatus est.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְשַׁעְיָ֔הוּ קְח֖וּ דְּבֶ֣לֶת תְּאֵנִ֑ים וַיִּקְח֛וּ וַיָּשִׂ֥ימוּ עַֽל־הַשְּׁחִ֖ין וַיֶּֽחִי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias said: Bring me a lump of figs. And when they had brought it, and laid it upon his boil, he was healed.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Jesaia sanoi: tuokaat minulle fikunarypäle. Ja he toivat ja panivat paisuman päälle, ja hän tuli terveeksi.
verses.king_james: And Isaiah said, Take a lump of figs. And they took and laid it on the boil, and he recovered.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixerat autem Ezechias ad Isaiam : Quod erit signum, quia Dominus me sanabit, et quia ascensurus sum die tertia templum Domini ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר חִזְקִיָּ֙הוּ֙ אֶֽל־יְשַׁעְיָ֔הוּ מָ֣ה א֔וֹת כִּֽי־יִרְפָּ֥א יְהֹוָ֖ה לִ֑י וְעָלִ֛יתִי בַּיּ֥וֹם הַשְּׁלִישִׁ֖י בֵּ֥ית יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ezechias had said to Isaias: What shall be the sign that the Lord will heal me, and that I will go up to the temple of the Lord the third day?
verses.finnish_biblia: Hiskia sanoi Jesaialle: mikä merkki siihen on, että Herra minun parantaa, ja että minun pitää menemän kolmantena päivänä Herran huoneesen?
verses.king_james: And Hezekiah said unto Isaiah, What shall be the sign that the Lord will heal me, and that I shall go up into the house of the Lord the third day?

verses.latin_vulgate: Cui ait Isaias : Hoc erit signum a Domino quod facturus sit Dominus sermonem quem locutus est : vis ut ascendat umbra decem lineis, an ut revertatur totidem gradibus ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֣אמֶר יְשַׁעְיָ֗הוּ זֶה־לְּךָ֤ הָאוֹת֙ מֵאֵ֣ת יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּ֚י יַעֲשֶׂ֣ה יְהֹוָ֔ה אֶת־הַדָּבָ֖ר אֲשֶׁ֣ר דִּבֵּ֑ר הָלַ֤ךְ הַצֵּל֙ עֶ֣שֶׂר מַעֲל֔וֹת אִם־יָשׁ֖וּב עֶ֥שֶׂר מַעֲלֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias said to him: This shall be the sign from the Lord, that the Lord will do the word which he hath spoken: Wilt thou that the shadow go forward ten lines, or that it go back so many degrees?
verses.finnish_biblia: Jesaia sanoi: sinä saat tämän merkin Herralta, että Herra tekee mitä hän sanonut on. Pitääkö varjon käymän kymmenen piirtoa edeskäsin, taikka kymmenen piirtoa takaperin?
verses.king_james: And Isaiah said, This sign shalt thou have of the Lord, that the Lord will do the thing that he hath spoken: shall the shadow go forward ten degrees, or go back ten degrees?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait Ezechias : Facile est umbram crescere decem lineis : nec hoc volo ut fiat, sed ut revertatur retrorsum decem gradibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ יְחִזְקִיָּ֔הוּ נָקֵ֣ל לַצֵּ֔ל לִנְט֖וֹת עֶ֣שֶׂר מַעֲל֑וֹת לֹ֣א כִ֔י יָשׁ֥וּב הַצֵּ֛ל אֲחֹרַנִּ֖ית עֶ֥שֶׂר מַעֲלֽוֹת׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ezechias said: It is an easy matter for the shadow to go forward ten lines: and I do not desire that this be done, but let it return back ten degrees.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Hiskia sanoi: se on keviä, että varjo käy edeskäsin kymmenen piirtoa: ei, mutta että hän kävis kymmenen piirtoa takaperin.
verses.king_james: And Hezekiah answered, It is a light thing for the shadow to go down ten degrees: nay, but let the shadow return backward ten degrees.

verses.latin_vulgate: Invocavit itaque Isaias propheta Dominum, et reduxit umbram per lineas quibus jam descenderat in horologio Achaz, retrorsum decem gradibus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּקְרָ֛א יְשַׁעְיָ֥הוּ הַנָּבִ֖יא אֶל־יְהֹוָ֑ה וַיָּ֣שֶׁב אֶת־הַצֵּ֗ל בַּֽ֠מַּעֲל֠וֹת אֲשֶׁ֨ר יָרְדָ֜ה בְּמַעֲל֥וֹת אָחָ֛ז אֲחֹרַנִּ֖ית עֶ֥שֶׂר מַעֲלֽוֹת׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias, the prophet, called upon the Lord, and he brought the shadow ten degrees backwards by the lines, by which it had already gone down on the dial of Achaz.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin rukoili Jesaia Herraa, ja hän veti varjon kymmenen piirtoa takaperin, jotka hän oli käynyt edeskäsin Ahaksen säjärissä.
verses.king_james: And Isaiah the prophet cried unto the Lord: and he brought the shadow ten degrees backward, by which it had gone down in the dial of Ahaz.

verses.latin_vulgate: In tempore illo misit Berodach Baladan, filius Baladan, rex Babyloniorum, litteras et munera ad Ezechiam : audierat enim quod ægrotasset Ezechias.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: בָּעֵ֣ת הַהִ֡יא שָׁלַ֡ח בְּרֹאדַ֣ךְ בַּ֠לְאֲדָ֠ן בֶּֽן־בַּלְאֲדָ֧ן מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֛ל סְפָרִ֥ים וּמִנְחָ֖ה אֶל־חִזְקִיָּ֑הוּ כִּ֣י שָׁמַ֔ע כִּ֥י חָלָ֖ה חִזְקִיָּֽהוּ׃
verses.douay_rheims: At that time Berodach Baladan, the son of Baladan, king of the Babylonians, sent letters and presents to Ezechias: for he had heard that Ezechias had been sick.
verses.finnish_biblia: Silloin lähetti Berodak Baladan Baladanin poika Babelin kuningas kirjoituksen ja lahjoja Hiskialle; sillä hän oli kuullut Hiskian sairastuneeksi.
verses.king_james: At that time Berodach–baladan, the son of Baladan, king of Babylon, sent letters and a present unto Hezekiah: for he had heard that Hezekiah had been sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Lætatus est autem in adventu eorum Ezechias, et ostendit eis domum aromatum, et aurum et argentum, et pigmenta varia, unguenta quoque, et domum vasorum suorum, et omnia quæ habere poterat in thesauris suis. Non fuit quod non monstraret eis Ezechias in domo sua, et in omni potestate sua.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁמַ֣ע עֲלֵיהֶם֮ חִזְקִיָּ֒הוּ֒ וַיַּרְאֵ֣ם אֶת־כׇּל־בֵּ֣ית נְכֹתֹ֡ה אֶת־הַכֶּ֩סֶף֩ וְאֶת־הַזָּהָ֨ב וְאֶת־הַבְּשָׂמִ֜ים וְאֵ֣ת ׀ שֶׁ֣מֶן הַטּ֗וֹב וְאֵת֙ בֵּ֣ית כֵּלָ֔יו וְאֵ֛ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֥ר נִמְצָ֖א בְּאוֹצְרֹתָ֑יו לֹא־הָיָ֣ה דָבָ֗ר אֲ֠שֶׁ֠ר לֹֽא־הֶרְאָ֧ם חִזְקִיָּ֛הוּ בְּבֵית֖וֹ וּבְכׇל־מֶמְשַׁלְתּֽוֹ׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Ezechias rejoiced at their coming, and he shewed them the house of his aromatical spices, and the gold, and the silver, and divers precious odours, and ointments, and the house of his vessels, and all that he had in his treasures. There was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominions, that Ezechias shewed them not.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Hiskia kuuli heitä, ja osoitti heille koko tavarahuoneensa, hopian, kullan, kalliit yrtit, ja parhaan öljyn, ja koko asehuoneensa ja kaikki, mitä hänen tavaroissansa oli. Ja ei mitään ollut hänen huoneessansa ja kaikessa hänen vallassansa, jota ei Hiskia heille osoittanut.
verses.king_james: And Hezekiah hearkened unto them, and shewed them all the house of his precious things, the silver, and the gold, and the spices, and the precious ointment, and all the house of his armour, and all that was found in his treasures: there was nothing in his house, nor in all his dominion, that Hezekiah shewed them not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Venit autem Isaias propheta ad regem Ezechiam, dixitque ei : Quid dixerunt viri isti ? aut unde venerunt ad te ? Cui ait Ezechias : De terra longinqua venerunt ad me, de Babylone.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיָּבֹא֙ יְשַׁעְיָ֣הוּ הַנָּבִ֔יא אֶל־הַמֶּ֖לֶךְ חִזְקִיָּ֑הוּ וַיֹּ֨אמֶר אֵלָ֜יו מָ֥ה אָמְר֣וּ ׀ הָאֲנָשִׁ֣ים הָאֵ֗לֶּה וּמֵאַ֙יִן֙ יָבֹ֣אוּ אֵלֶ֔יךָ וַיֹּ֙אמֶר֙ חִזְקִיָּ֔הוּ מֵאֶ֧רֶץ רְחוֹקָ֛ה בָּ֖אוּ מִבָּבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias, the prophet, came to king Ezechias, and said to him: What said these men? or from whence came they to thee? And Ezechias said to him: From a far country, they came to me out of Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin tuli propheta Jesaia kuningas Hiskian tykö ja sanoi hänelle: mitä nämät miehet ovat sanoneet, ja kusta he ovat tulleet sinun tykös? Hiskia sanoi: he ovat tulleet kaukaiselta maalta, Babelista.
verses.king_james: Then came Isaiah the prophet unto king Hezekiah, and said unto him, What said these men? and from whence came they unto thee? And Hezekiah said, They are come from a far country, even from Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: At ille respondit : Quid viderunt in domo tua ? Ait Ezechias : Omnia quæcumque sunt in domo mea, viderunt : nihil est quod non monstraverim eis in thesauris meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֕אמֶר מָ֥ה רָא֖וּ בְּבֵיתֶ֑ךָ וַיֹּ֣אמֶר חִזְקִיָּ֗הוּ אֵ֣ת כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֤ר בְּבֵיתִי֙ רָא֔וּ לֹא־הָיָ֥ה דָבָ֛ר אֲשֶׁ֥ר לֹֽא־הִרְאִיתִ֖ם בְּאֹצְרֹתָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: And he said: What did they see in thy house? Ezechias said: They saw all the things that are in my house: There is nothing among my treasures that I have not shewed them.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hän sanoi: mitä he ovat nähneet sinun huoneessas? Hiskia sanoi: he ovat nähneet kaikki, mitä minun huoneessani on, ja ei ole mitään minun tavaroissani, jota en minä heille osoittanut ole.
verses.king_james: And he said, What have they seen in thine house? And Hezekiah answered, All the things that are in mine house have they seen: there is nothing among my treasures that I have not shewed them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit itaque Isaias Ezechiæ : Audi sermonem Domini :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֥אמֶר יְשַׁעְיָ֖הוּ אֶל־חִזְקִיָּ֑הוּ שְׁמַ֖ע דְּבַר־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And Isaias said to Ezechias: Hear the word of the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Niin sanoi Jesaia Hiskialle: kuule Herran sanaa:
verses.king_james: And Isaiah said unto Hezekiah, Hear the word of the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce dies venient, et auferentur omnia quæ sunt in domo tua, et quæ condiderunt patres tui usque in diem hanc, in Babylonem : non remanebit quidquam, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּה֮ יָמִ֣ים בָּאִים֒ וְנִשָּׂ֣א ׀ כׇּל־אֲשֶׁ֣ר בְּבֵיתֶ֗ךָ וַאֲשֶׁ֨ר אָצְר֧וּ אֲבֹתֶ֛יךָ עַד־הַיּ֥וֹם הַזֶּ֖ה בָּבֶ֑לָה לֹא־יִוָּתֵ֥ר דָּבָ֖ר אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: Behold the days shall come, that all that is in thy house, and that thy fathers have laid up in store unto this day, shall be carried into Babylon: nothing shall be left, saith the Lord.
verses.finnish_biblia: Katso, aika tulee, että kaikki sinun huoneestas ja kaikki, mitä sinun esi-isäs koonneet ovat tähän päivään asti, viedään pois Babeliin, ja ei jätetä mitään, sanoo Herra.
verses.king_james: Behold, the days come, that all that is in thine house, and that which thy fathers have laid up in store unto this day, shall be carried into Babylon: nothing shall be left, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed et de filiis tuis qui egredientur ex te, quos generabis, tollentur, et erunt eunuchi in palatio regis Babylonis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וּמִבָּנֶ֜יךָ אֲשֶׁ֨ר יֵצְא֧וּ מִמְּךָ֛ אֲשֶׁ֥ר תּוֹלִ֖יד (יקח) [יִקָּ֑חוּ] וְהָיוּ֙ סָרִיסִ֔ים בְּהֵיכַ֖ל מֶ֥לֶךְ בָּבֶֽל׃
verses.douay_rheims: And of thy sons also that shall issue from thee, whom thou shalt beget, they shall take away, and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja myös lapset, jotka sinusta tulevat, jotkas siität, otetaan kamaripalvelioiksi Babelin kuninkaan huoneesen.
verses.king_james: And of thy sons that shall issue from thee, which thou shalt beget, shall they take away; and they shall be eunuchs in the palace of the king of Babylon.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dixit Ezechias ad Isaiam : Bonus sermo Domini quem locutus es : sit pax et veritas in diebus meis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיֹּ֤אמֶר חִזְקִיָּ֙הוּ֙ אֶֽל־יְשַׁעְיָ֔הוּ ט֥וֹב דְּבַר־יְהֹוָ֖ה אֲשֶׁ֣ר דִּבַּ֑רְתָּ וַיֹּ֕אמֶר הֲל֛וֹא אִם־שָׁל֥וֹם וֶאֱמֶ֖ת יִהְיֶ֥ה בְיָמָֽי׃
verses.douay_rheims: Ezechias said to Isaias: The word of the Lord, which thou hast spoken, is good: let peace and truth be in my days.
verses.finnish_biblia: Hiskia sanoi Jesaialle: Herran sana on hyvä, jonkas puhunut olet; ja sanoi vielä: eikö se niin ole? kuitenkin että rauha ja uskollisuus minun aikanani olis.
verses.king_james: Then said Hezekiah unto Isaiah, Good is the word of the Lord which thou hast spoken. And he said, Is it not good, if peace and truth be in my days?

verses.latin_vulgate: Reliqua autem sermonum Ezechiæ, et omnis fortitudo ejus, et quomodo fecerit piscinam et aquæductum, et introduxerit aquas in civitatem, nonne hæc scripta sunt in libro sermonum dierum regum Juda ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְיֶ֨תֶר דִּבְרֵ֤י חִזְקִיָּ֙הוּ֙ וְכׇל־גְּב֣וּרָת֔וֹ וַאֲשֶׁ֣ר עָשָׂ֗ה אֶת־הַבְּרֵכָה֙ וְאֶת־הַתְּעָלָ֔ה וַיָּבֵ֥א אֶת־הַמַּ֖יִם הָעִ֑ירָה הֲלֹא־הֵ֣ם כְּתוּבִ֗ים עַל־סֵ֛פֶר דִּבְרֵ֥י הַיָּמִ֖ים לְמַלְכֵ֥י יְהוּדָֽה׃
verses.douay_rheims: And the rest of the acts of Ezechias, and all his might, and how he made a pool, and a conduit, and brought waters into the city, are they not written in the book of the words of the days of the kings of Juda?
verses.finnish_biblia: Mitä enempi sanomista on Hiskiasta, ja hänen voimastansa, ja mitä hän tehnyt on, ja vesilammikosta ja kanavista, joilla hän vedet johdatti kaupunkiin: eikö se ole kirjoitettu Juudan kuningasten aikakirjassa?
verses.king_james: And the rest of the acts of Hezekiah, and all his might, and how he made a pool, and a conduit, and brought water into the city, are they not written in the book of the chronicles of the kings of Judah?

verses.latin_vulgate: Dormivitque Ezechias cum patribus suis, et regnavit Manasses filius ejus pro eo.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וַיִּשְׁכַּ֥ב חִזְקִיָּ֖הוּ עִם־אֲבֹתָ֑יו וַיִּמְלֹ֛ךְ מְנַשֶּׁ֥ה בְנ֖וֹ תַּחְתָּֽיו׃ {פ}
verses.douay_rheims: And Ezechias slept with his fathers, and Manasses, his son reigned in his stead.
verses.finnish_biblia: Ja Hiskia nukkui isäinsä kanssa; ja Manasse hänen poikansa tuli kuninkaaksi hänen siaansa.
verses.king_james: And Hezekiah slept with his fathers: and Manasseh his son reigned in his stead.
